data.formula: v += û · (1 − d/r)² · S · 0.5   (+ orbital swirl)
data.desc: a soft gravity-like well, optionally bent into a spiral
data.summary: Pulls matter into a well — an inverse-square gravity well bent into a spiral.
data.example: A hero word that pulls the field into orbit around it.

data.desc: soft outward push — carves a void
data.summary: Pushes matter away — an inverted well that carves a clean void.
data.example: A dismiss control that opens a clean void in the field.

data.desc: tangential swirl with light inward retention
data.summary: Spins matter into a whirlpool — a tangential force that circles without collapsing.
data.example: A loading state that spins the field into a swirl.

data.desc: a steady directional current along a heading
data.summary: Blows a directional current — a constant push along a fixed heading.
data.example: A scroll cue that combs the field downwind like a current.

data.desc: viscosity — thickens the medium, bleeding momentum
data.summary: Thickens the medium — viscosity that bleeds momentum off.
data.example: A modal backdrop the field thickens behind — motion settles.

data.formula: feed: v += û·(1 − d/r)²·(0.25 + 0.15·S);  at the nozzle (<24px): relaunch as a jet
data.desc: a conduit — draws matter in, jets it out along a heading
data.summary: A fountain — draws matter in, then jets it back out along a heading.
data.effect: Recycles the field into a stream.
data.example: A call-to-action that draws matter in and jets it back out.

data.formula: v += û · (d − rest) · k;  v *= 0.985,  rest = 0.6·r
data.desc: a tether with a rest length — holds matter at a shell radius
data.summary: A tether with a rest length — holds matter at a fixed radius.
data.example: A pinned element the field tethers to at a rest radius.

data.formula: inside box: push out the shallower axis, v_axis ← −0.85·v_axis
data.desc: an axis-aligned bouncing wall — sparks on hard impact
data.summary: A surface that bounces — an elastic collision off a bounding box.
data.example: A card the field bounces off — a wall that sparks on impact.

data.formula: capture within absorbR; at capacity, release everything (supernova)
data.desc: captures matter, holds it (conserved), then releases it
data.summary: Swallows matter and holds it — accretion, then supernova at the cap.
data.example: A cart that accretes matter, then releases it on checkout (a supernova).

data.formula: F = G·M·d̂ / (d² + ε²),  ε = r_s = 2GM/c²;  |v| ≤ c
data.desc: true softened inverse-square — a real 1/d² law
data.summary: A true inverse-square well — the real GM/d² law, softened at the core.
data.example: A heavy headline with a true 1/d² pull — a real well.

data.formula: F = −σ·q·GM·d̂ / (d² + ε²)
data.desc: the signed sibling of gravity — like repels, opposite attracts
data.summary: Coulomb attraction or repulsion — inverse-square, set by sign.
data.example: Two states that demix charged matter into +/− domains.

data.formula: v ← rotate(v, θ),  θ = q·spin·B·(1 − d/r)
data.desc: the Lorentz force — curves a moving charge, doing no work
data.summary: A force perpendicular to motion — bends a moving particle without doing work.
data.example: A region that curves moving charge into cyclotron arcs.

data.formula: v += √(2T) · ξ,  T = S·(1 − d/r),  ξ ~ N(0,1) per axis
data.desc: Langevin/Brownian agitation — a real temperature in the medium
data.summary: Brownian agitation — random kicks that heat the medium.
data.example: A "hot" badge that agitates the field with Brownian heat.

data.formula: overlapping, approaching discs exchange normal momentum (e = S)
data.desc: elastic pairwise collision — the hard-sphere billiard force
data.summary: Hard-sphere contact — particles bounce off one another.
data.example: A dense cluster where particles ricochet like billiards.

data.formula: deposit φ; v += ∇φ · S;  field blurs ∂φ/∂t = D∇²φ
data.desc: a pheromone field — deposit a mark and follow the diffused gradient
data.summary: Lays a trail and climbs it — matter follows the diffused mark up-gradient.
data.example: A trail particles lay down and follow — stigmergy.

data.formula: pulse φ at the source; v += r̂·|∇φ|·S (ride the front out);  ∂²φ/∂t² = c²∇²φ
data.desc: a travelling wave — particles ride the expanding front
data.summary: A travelling wave — a disturbance that moves through the field.
data.effect: Carries matter outward on the wavefront.
data.example: A button that fires a real shock wave across the field.

data.formula: M(x) += λ where matter sits;  v += û · (1 − d/r)² · S · 0.5 · (1 + μ·M)
data.desc: the field remembers — occupancy wears in paths that pull harder
data.summary: A trace of where matter has been — past paths persist and pull.
data.example: A frequently-used path that wears in and starts pulling harder.

data.formula: v ← rotate(v, θ_max · (1 − d/r) · sign)
data.desc: rotates velocity, preserving speed — bends paths without adding energy
data.summary: Bends passing tracks like refraction — focuses or scatters them.
data.example: A link that bends a passing flow into caustics — no capture.

data.formula: if v·n < 0:  v −= 2(v·n)·n
data.desc: a one-way membrane — passes along its heading, reflects the reverse
data.summary: A conditional aperture — passes some particles, blocks others.
data.effect: Filters the stream by a rule.
data.example: A one-way membrane: matter passes through but not back.

data.formula: v_y −= (ρ_med − ρ_p)·g,  ρ_p = base / (size·(1 + heat))
data.desc: a constant lift/sink by density — light matter rises, dense settles
data.summary: Lifts lighter matter and sinks heavier — density against a field.
data.example: A feed where light/hot items rise and dense ones settle.

data.formula: v∥ += S · (offset⊥ / r) · (1 − d/r)
data.desc: a laminar velocity gradient — flow grows with perpendicular offset
data.summary: Opposing currents at a boundary — a velocity gradient.
data.example: A divider with laminar flow — layers slide past each other.

data.formula: if heat < ½:  v += (node − p)·k;  v *= 0.9
data.desc: snaps cool matter onto a lattice; melts and frees it when hot
data.summary: Snaps matter onto a lattice — order grown from a seed.
data.example: A "cool" section where matter snaps onto a lattice.

data.formula: v += (ĥ·|v| − v)·k,  ĥ = neighbour-mean heading (or the body heading)
data.desc: steers toward a heading, preserving speed — flock alignment
data.summary: Turns matter to a common heading — flocking orientation.
data.example: A nav where particles flock to a shared heading.

data.formula: v += curl(ψ)·S,  ψ = sin(x·s + 0.2t)·cos(y·s − 0.2t)
data.desc: divergence-free curl-noise turbulence
data.summary: A broad, noisy current — turbulent drift across the field.
data.example: A hero with divergence-free turbulence drifting through it.

data.formula: neighbours: push if d < r₀, pull if r₀ < d < r₁
data.desc: short-range pressure + mid-range pull — surface tension
data.summary: Mutual attraction within a group — a surface tension.
data.example: A tag group that behaves like a droplet — a swarm with a skin.

data.formula: ρ = Σ W(d, h);  v += −k·(ρ − ρ₀)·∇W  (push down the density gradient)
data.desc: SPH density relaxation — incompressible even-fill via mutual repulsion
data.summary: Outward push from crowding — a density repulsion.
data.example: A dense cluster that relaxes to an even fill, like a settling fluid.

data.formula: predator: v += seek(nearest prey)·S;  prey: v += flee(nearest predator)·S
data.desc: two-species pursuit — predators seek prey, prey flee predators
data.summary: Tracks and chases a target — pursuit steering.
data.example: A two-species swarm that chases and scatters — a living ecosystem.

data.formula: while engaged: emit S·2 particles/frame along the heading, each living ~90 frames
data.desc: a source [S] — creates matter along the heading, budgeted by a lifespan + pool ceiling
data.summary: Seeds new matter at a source — emission of fresh particles.
data.example: A nozzle that jets matter into the field — a literal fountain or a star seeding the void.

data.formula: neighbours: e = d − L;  v += ½k·(e/L)·û  (holds rest length L = range·0.35)
data.desc: a Verlet distance constraint — ropes, chains, cloth, soft structures
data.summary: Binds two bodies with a constraint — rigid or elastic.
data.example: A cluster that holds together like cloth — a soft structure that ropes and drapes.

data.formula: assign p → target t (hash of gx);  v += (t − p)·k + jitter·(1 − arrived)
data.desc: matter assembles into a mark / chart / logo — never words (§11)
data.summary: Matter assembles into a mark — a logo or chart grown from drifting particles, never words.
data.example: A logo or chart that assembles from drifting matter — a mark, never words.

data.formula: modifier: scales sibling S by S(t) = 1 + sin(ω·t)
data.desc: pulses its sibling forces with a time-varying strength
data.summary: A modifier that breathes — scales its sibling forces with a pulsing strength.
data.example: Pair with attract: a well that breathes (pulsing strength).

data.formula: modifier: gates siblings outside an angular cone of the heading
data.desc: a directional gate — confines sibling forces to a beam
data.summary: A modifier cone — confines its sibling forces to a directed beam.
data.effect: Beams a sibling force into a cone.
data.example: Pair with stream: a directed beam confined to a cone.

data.formula: modifier: other bodies' force on matter inside r is scaled by clamp(1 − S·(1 − d/r)², min, 1)
data.desc: a quiet zone — attenuates other bodies' forces inside its radius (shield, never global)
data.summary: A quiet zone — other bodies' forces fade inside its radius, a shield of calm.
data.effect: Damps neighbors' forces inside its radius.
data.example: A reading column the field quiets down around — text shielded from a noisy page.

data.formula: on overlap: c_p ← mix(c_p, tint, rate);  the color advects with matter
data.desc: conserved color transport — matter takes on and carries a tint
data.summary: Carries color through the medium — a dye that mixes.
data.example: A section that stains passing matter its own color, carried away.

data.formula: n̂ = netField/|netField|;  v += (n̂·|v| − v)·k_steer + n̂·gain·a_accel,  gain = S·(1 − d/r)
data.desc: follow the field lines — steer onto and stream down the net field a body radiates
data.summary: Follows the field lines — matter streams along the field a body radiates.
data.effect: Streams matter along the field lines.
data.example: A magnet or charge whose field lines the swarm threads, like plasma along a solar prominence.

data.formula: within absorbR: p ← pairCentre + R(twist)·û·(absorbR·scale + 6);  v ← R(twist)·v
data.desc: a wormhole throat — relocates matter (conserved) to its data-pair body, twisted/scaled
data.summary: Relocates matter to a paired throat — a conserved wormhole, not a source or sink.
data.effect: Teleports matter from one throat to its pair.
data.example: A pair of portals: matter that enters one throat emerges from its partner, conserved.

data.intent: make important elements feel naturally weighted without shouting
keep related options moving around the active item
let search results settle by relevance, confidence, and recency
show information flowing through citations, dependencies, or routes
show how sources support, weaken, or contradict a claim
make contradiction, uncertainty, and unstable state visible
keep related elements visually and behaviorally connected
group related terms, cards, or sections without hard layout changes
show whether a form, workflow, or dataset is becoming stable
make long content pages reveal attention, memory, and concept links
show where a user has been, paused, returned, or accumulated attention
let stale, temporary, or completed state fade gracefully
show a state transition: draft → published, pending → complete, hidden → active
move particles or attention along field lines, relationships, or paths
reveal field lines, causality, prediction, topology, energy, and overlays
convert motion-heavy behavior into static, semantic, reduced-motion equivalents
show system urgency, density, and activity across a dashboard
make routes, breadcrumbs, and likely next paths feel connected
bind citations, footnotes, evidence, references into visible relationships
show validation as coherence instead of error noise
let command palettes settle around likely intent
leave a subtle trail of user interaction and selection
show schedule density, conflicts, and open space
reveal constraints, blockers, and coupled states
make outdated content, data, or files cool and recede
show confidence, verification, and unsupported claims
let finished work release pressure and settle into memory
let related cards, assets, or controls cluster intelligently
show accumulated instability without harsh alert patterns
show ownership or state moving from one person or system to another
reveal nearby relevant context around a focused element
teach field-ui by revealing DOM, bodies, fields, metrics, feedback, and overlays
let a document or app reveal which concepts carry the most weight
let opposing states, tags, or preferences sort themselves visibly
show how multiple sources gather around claims, topics, or decisions
bring wandering attention or unstable layout back into coherence
highlight elements that ring with the user current intent
slow risky, destructive, or irreversible actions without modal noise
make safe zones, drop zones, scopes, and containers perceptible
reveal meaningful state changes only when a threshold is crossed
show delay, loading, sync, and distributed system response as waves
preserve the origin and transformation history of content or data
surface items needing review before they decay, expire, or block work
align objects, cards, or text fragments by meaning instead of only geometry
teach the interface quietly based on hesitation, return, and attention
temporarily reveal hidden connections without changing layout
let importance rise and fall over time, workload, or context
show exactly what a recipe will register, measure, write, and render before enabling it
show collaborators as live influence, not static avatars
let agreement gather around stable options or decisions
make unresolved conflict visible without turning it into noise
show the downstream impact of a change across a system
make access, scope, and protected regions legible
reveal approaching risk before it becomes an error
pull likely actions toward the user's current context
stabilize multi-step flows around milestones
show which version, branch, or draft is becoming canonical
bind reviewers, comments, issues, and artifacts into one field
surface unusual behavior as local heat and instability
reveal what a component, recipe, or action can affect
help users tune settings toward a stable target
add resistance when movement would break meaning
guide users back from error, drift, or interrupted state
show the living rhythm of a product, workflow, or data system

data.name: blackhole
data.desc: a well, an accretion disk, an event horizon, and lensing

data.name: whitehole
data.desc: an emission horizon that throws matter out

data.name: star
data.desc: hydrostatic equilibrium — gravity balanced by thermal pressure

data.name: quasar
data.desc: an accreting black hole with polar jets

data.name: galaxy
data.desc: a spiral disk that settles into a plane

data.name: nebula
data.desc: a warm, slow cloud with rising wisps

data.name: tornado
data.desc: a funnel with an updraft, calmed at the edges

data.name: fountain
data.desc: a class-[S] source jetting matter up, arcing home under gravity

data.measures: how much matter has gathered on a body
the body's share of the page's finite focus
crowding — local density gradient against a body
disorder of the local field (calm ↔ chaotic)
how aligned a body's neighbourhood is
support a body has accreted (the evidence metric)
how travelled the lanes through a body are

data.gloss: a bounded UI verb, not nature

data.name: @fundamental-engine/platform
data.role: binds the engine to the DOM
